title: AI Agent Visibility
riido_task: RIID-4720 [Contracts] AI Agent RBAC and visibility vocabulary
summary: AI Agent admin, owner, public, private, and visible-agent vocabulary is generated from API policy, schema, enum, DSL, IR, and OpenAPI fixtures.
required_policy_rules: admin: read public and private agents, update all, delete all
owner: read owned agents, update owned agents, delete owned agents
user: read owned agents and other users' public agents only
invariants: admin may read public and private agents and may update or delete all agents.
owner may read, update, and delete owned agents.
user may read owned agents and other users' public agents only.
AgentVisibility is a closed public/private enum in generated client contracts.

loop.observation: Admin/owner/public/private visibility vocabulary lived in policy prose while DSL policy, schemas, enum, and query operations already defined the machine-readable contract.
loop.hypothesis: A focused visibility verifier can make RBAC vocabulary drift observable without expanding mutation-safety or thumbnail scope.
loop.execute: Verify RBAC policy rules, visibility enum values, visible-agent response schemas, RBAC-bound query operations, DSL/IR tuple parity, and OpenAPI projection.
loop.evaluate: CI fails when RBAC rules, public/private enum values, schema identity fields, generated reader, or v1/v2 visibility query projections drift.
loop.retrospective: Future visibility semantics should update API policy/schema fixtures and this manifest before reader text changes.
